patient.query: I’ve been feeling pretty overwhelmed lately. Sometimes, I just get this nagging thought that I'm not good enough, and it really brings me down. It’s hard to shake off, you know?

patient.query: I think they come from a lot of different places. Maybe from my childhood? I remember always feeling like I had to be perfect, like anything less was just... not acceptable. It makes me anxious when I think of stepping out of my comfort zone. It’s like this voice in my head telling me I’ll mess up, and then I just... freeze.

patient.query: I know it might not be fair, but it just feels real to me. Like, I remember times when I’ve messed up and the looks people gave me... or even just feeling like I let them down. It sticks with me. I suppose I don’t really give people a chance to show me that they might understand or be supportive. I just kind of assume the worst, you know?

triggers: feeling like I let them down

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: I don’t know... part of me wants to believe it could be different, but then I feel this wave of doubt. Like, what if I try and it turns out just as bad as before? It’s hard to picture anything going well. I guess I just worry that I won’t be able to handle it if it doesn’t.

I just worry that I won’t be able to handle it if it doesn’t.

patient.query: I suppose the evidence is just all those times in the past when I didn't measure up... I guess it would be actually succeeding and feeling good about myself for once. But that feels so far out of reach sometimes.
